title: limonium oleifolium subsp. opulentum
intro: Limonium oleifolium subsp. opulentum, also known as sea lavender, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Plumbaginaceae family. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of sea lavender include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, sea lavender is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
